data.title: Stellantis offers discounts on certain models in France to regain volume
BHP could face up to $2B hit after China restricts Jimblebar iron ore, Goldman Sachs says
data.description:
BHP Group (BHP) may incur losses of up to $2 billion due to pricing pressures after China limited imports of its Jimblebar iron ore, with discounts widening and lump premiums plunging, Goldman Sachs Group said.

The restrictions could lead to about $1 billion a year in losses from incremental discounts on the company’s major iron ore fines products at spot prices, Bloomberg News reported, citing a Jan. 27 report.

A further $1 billion could be added as an impact to revenue from discounts on lump products, with Newman Lump discounts driving an 80% fall in premiums paid on the product.

BHP earlier this month said it was hammering out annual contract terms with state iron ore buyer China Mineral Resources Group (CMRG). It flagged that it is looking to sell more of its products in other markets.

"During negotiations, we continue to optimise product placement distribution channels and take actions within our operations to preserve operational flexibility and productivity," BHP said in a statement. "This has seen some impact to realised price."

data.title: Landstar signals $569M heavy haul record as AI investments accelerate network growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Landstar System, Inc. (LSTR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Frank Lonegro, President and CEO, reflected on his first two years, highlighting the company's resilience during an extended freight recession and outlining five strategic priorities, with a strong focus on agent and BCO empowerment. He stated, "We doubled down on the company's strategic growth initiatives with 2 of those, heavy haul and U.S.-Mexico cross-border, representing approximately 20% of our business. While the cross-border business has been impacted by geopolitics, we are more than ready to leverage our new cross-border leadership as well as our strong agent presence and market position when the environment improves."
  • Lonegro reported that Landstar's heavy haul segment set a new revenue record at $569 million for fiscal 2025, up approximately 14% over the previous year. He emphasized the company’s commitment to technology, stating, "Approximately 50% of our IT CapEx budget for 2026 is dedicated to AI enablement and solutions."
  • On capital returns, Lonegro said, "Over the last 2 years, Landstar returned approximately $261 million to shareholders in the form of share repurchases and another $245 million in cash dividends."
  • CFO James Todd addressed discrete insurance and claim expenses, noting, "The 2025 fourth quarter financial results were negatively impacted by several discrete items impacting insurance and claims expense," including pretax charges related to vehicular accidents and a court judgment, as well as increased claim reserves.
  • VP and Chief Corporate Sales, Strategy & Specialized Freight Officer, James Applegate, discussed the company’s AI strategy, highlighting the focus on enabling growth for agents and BCOs through automation and enhanced digital tools, and the deployment of an AI-powered fraud detection solution.

Outlook

  • The company is providing revenue commentary rather than formal guidance due to market uncertainty. Lonegro explained, "The number of loads hauled via truck in January was approximately 1% below January 2025... while revenue per load in January was approximately 4% above January 2025."
  • Todd added, "We estimate that in the event fiscal February and fiscal March truck revenue per load outperformed normal seasonality, in line with the outperformance we experienced in fiscal January, the sequential revenue change experienced during the 2026 first quarter could be down low single digits versus the fourth quarter of 2025."

Financial Results

  • Truck transportation revenue in Q4 was nearly flat year-over-year, with a slight overall revenue decrease due to ocean revenue decline. Excluding contributions from Landstar Metro and prior agent fraud, total revenue decreased approximately 1% year-over-year.
  • Heavy haul revenue reached $170 million in Q4, reflecting a 23% increase year-over-year, with a 16% increase in revenue per load and a 7% increase in volume.
  • Gross profit for Q4 was $85.6 million, with a gross profit margin of 7.3%. Variable contribution was $166 million, or 14.1% of revenue.
  • Insurance and claim costs in Q4 rose to $56.1 million, up from $30.1 million in Q4 2024, primarily due to discrete accident-related charges and increased reserves.
  • The company ended the quarter with $452 million in cash and short-term investments and returned $180 million in share repurchases and $125 million in dividends in 2025.

Q&A

  • Jason Seidl, TD Cowen, asked about BCO utilization amid winter storms. Lonegro stated, "We certainly have had folks with a little bit of equipment challenges," but expressed confidence in recovery, while Matt Miller noted BCO utilization was up 8% year-over-year in Q4.
  • Seidl also questioned AI’s impact. Lonegro responded, "Where you're going to see the benefit for us is not going to be on the cost line... what we're doing and going to do is enable the agent offices... to work smarter and to work faster."
  • Paul Stoddard, Goldman Sachs, focused on BCO count and variable contribution margin. Miller said, "Net truck count declined 104 trucks in the quarter... gross truck adds were up 8.9%... gross truck cancels are down 5.1%."
  • Bascome Majors, Susquehanna, asked about expense pacing. Todd cited a potential $12 million headwind in incentive compensation if plan targets are met.
  • Stephanie Moore, Jefferies, probed for market green shoots. Lonegro highlighted, "We saw a sequential improvement month-over-month in the quarter. And so far, as JT mentioned, when you look at how we're trending in January, that seems to have a little bit of sustainability to us."
  • Andrew Cox, Stifel, asked about AI tool adoption. Lonegro and Applegate detailed agent segmentation and the importance of voluntary tool uptake, with Applegate adding, "We've got resources, we've got beta agents... as AI comes about, we've got resources, we've got beta agents."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was cautiously optimistic, with questions probing risks, margin potential, and AI’s impact, yet showing interest in signs of stabilization or growth.
  • Management’s tone was confident and constructive in prepared remarks, emphasizing resilience, technology investments, and strategic growth, though more measured and detailed on challenges and headwinds during Q&A.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management showed increased confidence regarding technology deployment and heavy haul performance, but remained guarded due to insurance and claims volatility.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Heavy haul revenue growth accelerated from 17% in Q3 to 23% in Q4, reaching a new annual record.
  • Insurance and claims costs rose sharply in Q4 due to specific accident-related charges versus Q3’s more routine claims development.
  • AI investments increased, with 50% of 2026 IT CapEx dedicated to AI enablement, compared to broader digital transformation in Q3.
  • Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 (noting "modest sequential pricing improvement") to more assertive confidence in technology and heavy haul, but remained realistic about persistent macro pressures.
  • Analysts’ focus moved from market capacity and regulatory risks in Q3 to AI strategy, margin drivers, and BCO productivity in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified insurance and claim costs as a significant Q4 headwind, citing $11 million in accident-related charges, a $5.7 million court judgment, and a $5.3 million increase in claim reserves.
  • The company is monitoring winter storm impacts on BCO utilization and overall loadings, with recovery dependent on weather normalization.
  • Ongoing sale of Landstar Metro and exposure to geopolitical factors in cross-border business were noted as areas of uncertainty.
  • Analysts highlighted risks in BCO retention, compensation headwinds, and adoption pace of AI tools.

Final Takeaway

Landstar closed the year with a record heavy haul performance and a significant shift in technology investments toward AI to empower its network and drive future growth. Management maintains a focus on core strengths in safety, service, and agent-led growth while navigating insurance cost headwinds and ongoing freight market challenges, positioning the company to capitalize when demand recovers and new efficiencies take hold.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Viavi anticipates $386M–$400M Q3 revenue with continued data center and aerospace momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Viavi Solutions (VIAV) Q2 2026

Management View

  • President and CEO Oleg Khaykin stated that "the second quarter of fiscal '26 came in at the high end of our guidance, driven by strong growth in many of our end markets." He highlighted that data center ecosystem revenue momentum is expected to continue through 2026, noting robust demand from high-performance semiconductors, optical modules, and hyperscalers for lab and field products as AI data center build-outs accelerate.
  • Khaykin reported: "Our Aerospace and Defense business also saw another strong quarter of growth, driven by continued high demand for our positioning, navigation and timing products. We expect this trend to continue through the rest of the calendar year."
  • CFO Ilan Daskal announced, "Net revenue for the quarter was $369.3 million, which is at the high end of our guidance range of $360 million and $370 million. Revenue was up 23.5% sequentially and on a year-over-year basis was up 36.4%." Daskal also stated, "EPS at $0.22 was also above the high end of our guidance range of $0.18 to $0.20 and was up $0.07 sequentially."
  • Daskal outlined a restructuring plan impacting approximately 5% of the global workforce, estimating $32 million in restructuring charges and annual savings of about $30 million. He explained that "we intend to reinvest a portion of these savings with higher growth areas of our business."

Outlook

  • Daskal guided for third fiscal quarter 2026 revenue in the range of $386 million and $400 million. He expects total NSE revenue between $304 million and $316 million, OSP revenue in the range of $82 million and $84 million, operating margin for Viavi at 19.7% plus or minus 50 basis points, and EPS between $0.22 and $0.24.
  • The company expects to recognize most restructuring charges by June 2026 and complete the plan by December 2026, with projected annual savings to benefit operating expenses.
  • Khaykin stated, "Given strong and growing customer demand, we expect the data center ecosystem revenue momentum to continue through the calendar 2026."

Financial Results

  • Net revenue for Q2 was $369.3 million. Operating margin reached 19.3%. EPS was $0.22. Cash and short-term investments at the end of Q2 were $772.1 million. Cash flow from operating activities was $42.5 million. CapEx for the quarter was $5.6 million.
  • NSE revenue for Q2 was $291.5 million and OSP revenue was $77.8 million. NSE gross margin was 64.7%, while OSP gross margin was 50.8%.
  • The company exchanged about $100 million in convertible notes for 7.9 million shares and prepaid $100 million of its $600 million Term Loan B.
  • Fully diluted share count for the quarter was 233.4 million shares.

Q&A

  • Ruben Roy, Stifel: Asked about the evolving mix in the NSE business and guidance drivers. Khaykin explained the mix shift, stating that "we are now only about 40%, a little bit under 40% exposed to service provider, traditional telecom service provider. And I'd say 60% is driven by the data center ecosystem and the aerospace and defense."
  • Roy, Stifel: Inquired about visibility and order backlog in data center given industry partnerships. Khaykin replied, "when it comes to data center, I would say, for us, traditionally, we only had like maybe 1, 1.5 quarter visibility. we have a pretty good view at least on the base demand from these type of activities up to 3 quarters ahead."
  • Roy, Stifel: Questioned the focus of workforce restructuring. Daskal responded, "it's across multiple functions just to make sure that we have operate under the much higher efficiency. So it's not targeting specific areas."
  • Ryan Koontz, Needham: Asked about opportunities in defense and aerospace. Khaykin described growth in "resilient PNT, position and navigation timing," with immediate demand coming from autonomous systems and drones.
  • Andrew Spinola, UBS: Sought color on data center revenue breakdown. Khaykin explained, "we don't break those things individually because given any quarter, the mix may be a bit up or down... we just call it generally data center ecosystem."
  • Tim Savageaux, Northland: Asked about Spirent's sequential contribution. Daskal noted, "seasonality for the Spirent business, usually, the first half of the calendar year is the weaker part and the stronger part is the second half."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked detailed questions on segment mix, order visibility, and the impact of industry partnerships, indicating a positive to neutral tone, with focus on sustained growth and business mix evolution.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing ongoing momentum in core markets. Khaykin used phrases such as "we expect this trend to continue" and "we are seeing... very positive development."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence increased, with more pronounced forward-looking statements about multi-quarter visibility and continued data center strength. Analysts remained constructive but pressed for more detail on segment contributions and restructuring.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 2026 is higher than Q2, with revenue now guided at $386 million to $400 million compared to Q2’s $369.3 million result and prior Q2 guidance of $360 million to $370 million.
  • Mix shift in NSE continues: service provider exposure has declined to below 40% from 45% the previous quarter, while data center and aerospace/defense combined now represent about 60%.
  • Management commentary shifted to highlight longer order visibility (up to three quarters) in the data center segment, compared to 1–1.5 quarters previously.
  • The restructuring plan is a new development this quarter, with cost savings to be reinvested in high-growth areas.

Risks and Concerns

  • Daskal noted that OSP operating margin was slightly below guidance due to higher variable costs.
  • The company expects to incur approximately $32 million in restructuring charges, with potential operational impacts as workforce adjustments are carried out.
  • Spirent revenue was slightly below expectations due to the timing of certain opportunities, and its first-half seasonality may weigh on near-term results.

Final Takeaway

Viavi Solutions highlighted robust sequential and year-over-year growth in Q2 2026, with strong momentum in data center and aerospace/defense markets. The company signaled confidence in its growth trajectory, underpinned by longer-term order visibility and a focused shift toward higher-growth segments. Strategic restructuring is expected to generate significant cost savings, further enabling reinvestment in core growth areas. Management’s tone reflected increased confidence in sustaining demand through 2026, while analysts probed for additional clarity on segment performance, order dynamics, and the impact of recent industry developments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SAP SE Non-GAAP EPS of €1.62 beats by €0.11, revenue of €9.68B misses by €80M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• SAP SE press release (SAP): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of €1.62 beats by €0.11.
  • Revenue of €9.68B (+3.2% Y/Y) misses by €80M.
  • In the fourth quarter, current cloud backlog grew by 16% to €21.05 billion and was up 25% at constant currencies. 
  • Q4 was a strong cloud quarter, with bookings resulting in 30% Total Cloud Backlog growth to a record €77 billion.Total cloud backlog up 22% and up 30% at constant currencies.
  • Cloud revenue was up 19% to €5.61 billion and up 26% at constant currencies. Cloud ERP Suite revenue was up 23% to €4.86 billion and up 30% at constant currencies.
  • Non-IFRS operating profit up 28% and up 31% at constant currencies in FY2025.
  • SAP announces a new, two-year share repurchase program with a volume of up to €10 billion.
  • Operating cash flow in the fourth quarter increased from -€0.58 billion to €1.30 billion and free cash flow increased from -€0.91 billion to €1.03 billion. 

  • Outlook 2026

    Financial Outlook 2026For 2026, SAP expects:

    • €25.8 – 26.2 billion cloud revenue at constant currencies (2025: €21.02 billion), up 23% to 25% at constant currencies.
    • €36.3 – 36.8 billion cloud and software revenue at constant currencies (2025: €32.54 billion), up 12% to 13% at constant currencies.
    • €11.9 – 12.3 billion non-IFRS operating profit at constant currencies (2025: €10.42 billion), up 14% to 18% at constant currencies.
    • Approximately €10 billion free cash flow at actual currencies (2025: €8.24 billion).
    • An effective tax rate (non-IFRS) of approximately 29% (2025: 30.4%)[3].
    • Constant currency current cloud backlog growth to slightly decelerate in 2026 (2025: 25%).

    SAP further expects:

    • Constant currency total revenue growth to accelerate through 2027.
    • Total operating expenses to grow at 80% to 90% of total revenue growth in 2027.
    • Constant currency software support revenue decline rate to accelerate in the coming years as a consequence of an acceleration of customers transforming to the cloud.

data.title: Asia markets mixed as tech rally cools following Fed hold and mixed earnings
data.description:
Asian stock markets slowed down on Thursday. Technology companies saw their prices drop after a strong run, as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve’s decision to keep interest rates the same and a mix of good and bad financial results from major U.S. tech firms.

Gold extended its record-breaking rally above $5,500 per ounce on Thursday, hitting a fresh high amid continued weakness in the US dollar and heightened economic and geopolitical uncertainty.

Singapore’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate held steady at 2% in Q4 2025, unchanged from the previous quarter, preliminary estimates showed. Singapore’s Domestic Supply Price Index dropped by 3.3% year-on-year in December 2025, reversing an upwardly revised 2.8% increase in the previous month.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.14% to around 53,500 on Thursday, marking its third consecutive session of gains. The Japanese yen was around 153.2 per dollar on Thursday, following a nearly 1% decline in the previous session.

Traders remain cautious about the risk of unilateral action from Tokyo, although Bank of Japan data shows that authorities have not officially intervened in the market so far. Political uncertainty also weighed on the yen in the run-up to the Feb. 8 lower house snap election.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.02% to around 4,140 while the Shenzhen Component edged up 0.2% to 14,370 on Thursday.

In other news, Goldman Sachs projects that Chinese equities could attract roughly $518 billion in new domestic capital this year, driven by strong corporate earnings and improving market sentiment.

Caution also set in ahead of China’s official January PMI release this weekend, following late-2025 gains in both manufacturing and services.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.50% to 27,789 in Thursday morning trade.

The Hong Kong Monetary Authority kept its base rate unchanged at 4.0% on January 29, 2026, following the U.S. Federal Reserve’s decision hours earlier to hold its target range at 3%–3.75%.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.52% to 81,752 in morning trade on Thursday, erasing gains from the previous two sessions as traders grew cautious ahead of the Union Budget 2026 due this weekend. 

However, a trade agreement between India and the EU capped the fall. Most sectors traded in the red, including auto, tech, banking, and financial services. India will immediately slash duties on high-end European cars to 30% from as high as 110% under its new trade deal with the EU, an official said, opening the tightly controlled market to luxury carmakers such as BMW and Mercedes-Benz, as Reuters reported.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.12% to around 8,890 in Thursday morning trade, extending losses from the previous session. The Australian dollar rose above $0.70 on Thursday, hitting a fresh three-year high, driven by surging gold prices and growing expectations of an imminent rate hike.

Australia’s export prices increased by 3.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 2025, recovering from a 0.9% decline in Q3. Australia’s import prices increased 0.9% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 2025, defying market expectations of a 0.2% decline and reversing a 0.4% fall in Q3.

The Philippines’ GDP grew 3% year-on-year in Q4 2025, below expectations of 3.8% and slowing from 3.9% in the previous quarter.

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended flat after the Federal Reserve held its rates unchanged, as largely expected, ahead of earnings results from major AI players after the closing bell.

U.S. stock futures fell slightly on Thursday as investors digested earnings from megacap tech firms alongside a widely expected decision by the Fed to hold rates steady.: Dow -0.06%; S&P 500 +0.15%; Nasdaq +0.33%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Norway’s $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund reduces stakes in top U.S. tech firms
data.description:
• Norway’s $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund reduced its holdings in major U.S. tech companies, including its top position in Nvidia (NVDA), during the second half of 2025, according to newly released portfolio data.
  • By year-end, Norges Bank Investment Management, which oversees the fund, had trimmed all of its top four tech holdings, which also included Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL).
  • The fund cut its stake in Nvidia to 1.26% from 1.32% earlier, while Microsoft's holdings were slashed to 1.26% from 1.35% earlier. The two companies still feature among the fund's most valued investments, with Apple (AAPL) retaining the second spot.
  • Overall, U.S. investments accounted for 52.9% of the fund’s assets at the end of 2025 across equities, bonds, and property, up from 52.4% six months earlier, the data showed.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Updating the goods portion of the advance report and offering initial data on services, this report provides complete information on cross-border trade.

Imports and the deficit are expected to snap back to show a deficit of $45 billion after tariff effects slashed imports, leading to a modest $29.4 billion deficit in October.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims are expected to be up to 205K from 200K, as observers see 200K as too low to represent current conditions.

8:30 AM Productivity and Costs

Productivity measures the growth of labor efficiency in producing the economy's goods and services.

Q3 is expected to be unrevised at 4.9 percent productivity growth with ULC unrevised at minus 1.9 percent.

10:00 AM Factory Orders

Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods.

Orders are expected to reverse the 1.3 percent drop of October with a 1.3 percent increase in November.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report 

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country.

1:00 PM 7-Yr Note Auction

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $44 billion of 7-year notes.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Home Depot chops 800 corporate jobs, orders staff back to office full-time
Singapore's GIC partners with Sony Music to acquire music catalog assets
data.description:
• Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund GIC has teamed up with Sony Music Group (SONY) to acquire music catalog assets, the companies said Thursday.
  • The statement said the partnership will combine the operational capabilities of Sony Music Group, the music arm of Japanese ‌conglomerate Sony Group (SONY), with GIC's long-term ​capital to manage potential assets.
  • Bloomberg News reported that the vehicle could include $2 billion ‍to $3 billion in investment. GIC and Sony did not provide an amount in the statement.
  • "The music ecosystem is a resilient sector with attractive long-term growth prospects and we are excited about the next stage of streaming monetization through premiumization and subscriber growth in emerging markets," Girish Karira, Head of Integrated Strategies Group, GIC said.
